g.data-narration: Introduction to morphological algorithms in image processing
Morphological algorithms analyze image shapes using set theory and a structuring element
g.text: Techniques for analyzing and manipulating shape/structure in images using
set theory and topology. Use a small structuring element to probe objects.

g.data-narration: The structuring element is a small template that probes the image pixel by pixel
g.text: A small template that probes the image. The SE slides across each pixel,
checking neighborhood relationships to determine output pixel values.

g.data-narration: Erosion shrinks objects by removing boundary pixels where the structuring element does not fully fit
g.text: Shrinks objects by removing boundary pixels. Pixel equals 1 only if ALL SE pixels
fall within the object. Formula: A minus B equals z such that B subset of A

g.data-narration: Dilation expands objects by adding boundary pixels where the structuring element overlaps
g.text: Expands objects by adding boundary pixels. Pixel equals 1 if ANY SE pixel overlaps
the object. Formula: A plus B equals z such that B intersection A is not empty

g.data-narration: Opening removes small protrusions while closing fills small holes
Hit-or-Miss transform detects specific patterns using foreground and background structuring elements
g.text: Detects specific patterns. Uses TWO structuring elements: one for foreground,
one for background matching. Result equals positions where pattern EXACTLY matches.

g.data-narration: Boundary extraction finds object edges by subtracting the eroded image from the original
g.text: Extract object boundaries by subtracting eroded image from original.
Boundary of A equals A minus erosion of A

g.data-narration: Region filling uses iterative conditional dilation to fill holes starting from a seed point
g.text: Fill holes starting from seed point inside the region. Iterative conditional dilation.
Xk equals (Xk-1 plus B) intersection complement of A

g.data-narration: Morphological gradient detects edges while top-hat extracts specific features from images
Skeletonization reduces objects to their medial axis while preserving topological properties
g.text: Reduces objects to their medial axis (skeleton) while preserving topology.
Useful for shape analysis, recognition, and path planning.

g.data-narration: Morphological operations are used in segmentation, recognition, filtering, and restoration
Erosion shrinks, dilation expands, opening smooths while removing noise, closing smooths while filling holes
